summary: Large match-progress PR adding C implementations and prototype cleanup across Kirby specials, game-mode/result logic, menu name handling, trophy input, and stage helpers. The PR reported 34 matched functions and 7 failed attempts, but was closed unmerged and explicitly replaced by PRs #2322, #2323, #2324, and #2325, so its main value is as a decomposition/matching work slice rather than a landed change.
what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments with matched implementations for several Kirby Neutral-B and copied-move helpers, including ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll, and fn_80105978.

Kirby Final Cutter enter functions now reset command and move variables, change to ground or air motion state, call ftAnim_8006EBA4, spawn effect 0x494, set x2219_b0, and install effect pause/resume hitlag callbacks.

Kirby inhale helpers compute an inhaled-position offset from cur_pos, facing_dir, and ftKb_DatAttrs, then either pull/swallow a target item through it_802F23AC/it_802F2810 or a victim fighter through ftCo_800BD19C/ftCo_800BD620 before transitioning based on GA_Air versus ground.

Kirby copied Jigglypuff Rollout collision code handles air-to-ground and ground collision transitions, reassigns callbacks after motion changes, transfers self velocity to ground velocity, and provides a turnaround helper that flips velocity, acceleration, facing-related move fields, and Y rotation.
Implemented gm_16F1.c fn_80170110 as a bonus-decision table lookup that checks flags against a mask and routes awards either to pl_80039418 for lower bonus kinds or fn_801701C0 for other kinds.
Implemented gm_1BA8.c gm_801BEDA8 to cycle CSS match_type backward or forward, wrapping through 24 match types, then copy CSS data into VsModeData.
Implemented gmregclear.c fn_8017F09C to compute a timer-based result bonus using gm_8016AE38 state, lbl_80472D28 fields, and a multiplier, clamping negative products to zero.
Renamed and exposed the Icicle Mountain helper as grIceMt_801F91EC with a typed signature, replacing a local FUN_801f91ec-style name and updating gricemt.h.
Adjusted grIceMt_801F91EC animation setup calls from a -20 argument to 0 while initializing joint tracking values and mpLib callbacks.
Implemented grKongo_801D8078 to scan HSD_GObj_Entities->items for item kind 0xDA and return a nearby item within grKg_804D6980->unk28 radius of a Ground_801C4DA0 position.
Implemented mnname.c IsNameUnique as a loop over up to 0x78 persistent name entries, checking IsNameValid, fetching GetPersistentNameData, and comparing through CompareNameStrings.
Implemented toy.c un_80305D00 to scan controller normalized stick X values across four pads and return the first value with absolute magnitude greater than 0.1F.

Updated headers to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete return and argument types for functions such as IsNameUnique, un_80305D00, fn_8017F09C, fn_80170110, fn_801701C0, and grIceMt_801F91EC.
smart_moves: Used concrete header prototype upgrades alongside implementations, reducing future ambiguity and making call sites type-checkable.
Kept ground and air versions of Kirby Final Cutter enter logic structurally parallel, changing only the motion state and ground/air behavior needed for matching.

For Kirby inhale, explicitly separated item and fighter distance helpers because the evidence shows different return types and APIs: it_802F23AC returns f64-like distance usage while ftCo_800BD19C returns f32.

Used local scoped reloads of Fighter* after Fighter_ChangeMotionState in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, matching the common decomp pattern where motion changes may require re-fetching fighter data for register allocation or correctness.
Preserved exact small constants and bit patterns such as 0x0C4C5092U, 0x494, 0x17, 24, 0x78, 0.1F, item kind 0xDA, and GA_Air/GA_Ground branches rather than abstracting them prematurely.

Renamed a decompiler-style FUN_801f91ec symbol to the project-style grIceMt_801F91EC while maintaining the address-derived suffix, improving searchability without inventing a semantic name beyond available evidence.
Added #pragma dont_inline around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, indicating an explicit matching tactic where inlining behavior affected codegen.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter move enter functions, reset cmd_vars and move-specific state fields in the original order; even equivalent chained assignment versus individual stores may matter for codegen, as shown by differing reset styles between ground and air Kirby Final Cutter.
After motion state changes, re-fetching GET_FIGHTER can help match register allocation and reflect project conventions for callbacks and state writes after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Distance comparisons in gameplay code often compare squared distance against squared velocity/radius; preserve the squared-threshold form to avoid introducing sqrt behavior or mismatched floating-point code.
Header work is part of matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact bool, s32, float, int, void*, callback, and struct pointer types can unlock or stabilize downstream matches.
Stage helper matches may require both signature correction and call-argument correction; grIceMt_801F91EC changed its exported prototype and the grAnime_801C7A04 argument from -20 to 0.
For item scans, iterate HSD_GObj_Entities->items directly and use item kind checks plus item-position APIs rather than assuming a typed container abstraction.
Menu and save-data helpers can have intentionally odd loop behavior; IsNameUnique breaks on the first invalid/null name data entry rather than continuing through all 0x78 slots.
Controller helper functions may return the last sampled raw value even if below threshold depending on loop structure; preserve ret assignment placement when matching.
naming_conventions: Stage functions use subsystem/name/address style such as grIceMt_801F91EC and grKongo_801D8078.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic game-mode functions retain fn_address style, e.g. fn_80170110 and fn_8017F09C.
Menu name functions can use semantic names when known, e.g. IsNameUnique, IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, GetPersistentNameData.
Kirby move functions use ftKb_ prefixes plus move/action names, e.g. ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll.
Headers should mirror concrete C signatures rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once an implementation is matched.
Avoid retaining Ghidra-style FUN_ names when a project address-name convention is known; this PR replaced FUN_801f91ec with grIceMt_801F91EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline was added before ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, showing an explicit tactic to control MWCC inlining/codegen.

The PR body notes a MWCC register allocation issue in ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim and ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim where GET_FIGHTER/getFighter helped fp allocation but not victim_gobj, requiring four callee-saved registers r28-r31; this is reported evidence, not visible as a final implementation in the excerpt.
Chained assignments versus separate assignments were used selectively for cmd_vars resets, suggesting statement shape was tuned to match generated stores/register allocation.
Scoped temporaries such as f64 dist and f32 dist in Kirby inhale helpers preserve return-type-specific codegen for item versus fighter APIs.
Manual decomposition of dx, dy, dz and squared components in grKongo_801D8078 preserves floating-point operation order for radius comparisons.
Using casts in it_802F23AC((int*) fp->target_item_gobj, (float*) &pos) reflects prototype or type-system limitations retained to match call codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only explicit human/project disposition was that PR #2321 was replaced by #2322, #2323, #2324, and #2325.
Because the PR was closed with merged_at null, treat this as an unmerged work-in-progress bundle rather than a landed canonical change.
follow_up_queries: Inspect replacement PRs #2322, #2323, #2324, and #2325 to identify which parts of this WIP bundle landed and whether implementations changed during review.
Search for ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim and ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim in later PRs to see how the noted MWCC callee-saved register allocation issue was resolved.
Verify whether grIceMt_801F91EC's grAnime_801C7A04 final argument stayed 0 in the replacement PRs.
Check later headers for final prototypes of it_802F23AC and ftCo_800BD19C to see whether casts in ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 were later removed.
Compare replacement PR diffs for IsNameUnique and un_80305D00 to confirm loop-break and return-value behavior were accepted.
